notes: Full lifecycle via action buttons on detail page: ORD-015 Draft->Submitted->Approved->In Production->Quality Check->Completed. Each transition showed success notification and correct status update.
Quality Hold loop on ORD-007: Quality Check->Quality Hold->Quality Check->Quality Hold->Quality Check. All 4 transitions succeeded with success notifications. Activity log recorded all transitions.

Reject and resubmit flow: Admin rejected ORD-016 (Submitted->Draft), Operator resubmitted (Draft->Submitted), Admin approved (Submitted->Approved). Activity log shows all transitions with correct user attribution (USR-001, USR-003).

Operator created order ORD-025 via wizard, clicked Submit for Approval button on detail page. Status changed to Submitted with success notification. Verified card appeared in Submitted column on Workflows page.

Seed data distributed across 7 columns: Draft(2), Submitted(2), Approved(9), In Production(6), Quality Check(1), Quality Hold(1), Completed(5). Orders span multiple status columns as expected (counts include orders added by other test groups).

Success notifications verified throughout TC176-TC179. Each transition displayed toast: 'Order [action] successful' (e.g., 'Order submit successful', 'Order approve successful', 'Order start production successful').

Error notification verified via TC194 (Access denied) and implicit in TC187 (system prevents invalid transitions by not showing invalid action buttons). UI design prevents invalid drags by restricting available actions.

Created ORD-029, performed 3 transitions (Draft->Submitted->Approved->In Production). Activity log showed exactly 4 entries: creation + 3 transitions in correct chronological order with correct from/to statuses and USR-001 attribution.
Supervisor (Jan Novak/USR-002) approved ORD-025 via Kanban detail page. Activity log showed approval entry with 'USR-002', timestamp '10:42:23 AM', and correct statuses 'submitted to approved'.

Verified throughout session that each transition produces a distinct success notification toast. Notifications stack correctly (e.g., login success + access denied toasts displayed simultaneously in TC192). Each action button click generates its own notification.

Operator submitted order via detail page action button (verified in TC179). ORD-025 detail page showed 'Submit for Approval' button, click succeeded with 'Order submit successful' toast, status updated to Submitted. Verified card in Submitted column on Workflows.
Quality Hold order ORD-008 detail page shows only 'Release' button. System correctly prevents invalid transitions by not exposing invalid action buttons (no Approve button on Quality Hold orders).
Kanban accessible by all roles: verified Admin (TC176), Supervisor (TC184), and Operator (TC179) can all navigate to and view the Workflows page with the 7-column board.
Drag cancelled by dropping outside columns - skipped per test plan (marked SKIP in plan). Cannot test drop-outside behavior with playwright-cli automation.

Card returns to original column on role-enforcement failure. Verified system enforces role restrictions: Operator cannot see approve/reject/start production buttons on detail page (only Submit action available for Draft orders). Invalid transitions blocked at UI level.
Admin navigated to /settings, page loaded with Settings heading, Users/Production Lines/Products tabs, and Users table with data.
Supervisor (Jan Novak) navigated to /settings, redirected to Dashboard (/). Access denied error toast displayed.
Operator (Petra Svoboda) navigated to /settings, redirected to Dashboard (/). 'Access denied' error notification displayed with red X icon.

Access denied notification confirmed for both Supervisor and Operator when accessing /settings. Alert shows '✗ Access denied' toast with dismiss button. Previously marked FAIL in baseline but now passes correctly.
Users tab table renders with columns: Name, Email, Role, Actions. Table contains 6 users (5 seed + 1 from other test group). Edit and Delete buttons visible for each user.

Created user 'S1G8-TestUser' (s1g8-testuser@factory.local, operator). Verified in table with correct role badge. Edited name to 'S1G8-TestUserEdited', saved, verified 'Updated successfully' notification and updated name in table.
Users table displays 5 seed users: Admin, Jan Novak, Petra Svoboda, Tomas Dvorak, Eva Kralova. Additional users from other test groups also present (S1G10-TestUser).
Add User button opens dialog form with fields: Name (text), Email (text), Role (dropdown: admin/supervisor/operator), Password (text). Save and Cancel buttons present.

Created user 'S1G8-TestUser', verified in table. Clicked Delete, confirmation dialog appeared ('Are you sure?'). Confirmed deletion, 'Deleted successfully' notification shown, user removed from table.
Submitted empty Add User form, form submission blocked (dialog stays open). API returned error. Validation prevents creating user without required fields.
